 {"id":"bd-2yn4","title":"Executor(FrankenSQLite): Apply OpLog via fsqlite API (with import/export bridge for real DB files)","description":"## Goal
Implement the FrankenSQLite executor for OpLogs.

## Reality Check (Current Engine State)
If FrankenSQLite cannot yet open arbitrary SQLite file-format DBs directly, this executor must support an **import/export bridge**:
- Import fixture by running `sqlite3 fixture.db .dump` and feeding the SQL into FrankenSQLite.
- Export final state as a canonical dump for comparison/canonicalization.

(When file-backed compatibility mode is available, we can add a direct-open fast path.)

## Concurrency Model
- Spawn N worker threads/tasks.
- Each worker uses its own `fsqlite::Connection` and executes its slice of the OpLog.
- Collect per-worker metrics (retries/aborts if applicable).

## Outputs
- Run report JSON (same schema as sqlite3 executor)
- Final dump artifact

## Acceptance
- Runs the same OpLog as sqlite3 executor and produces output artifacts.
- No panics; errors are captured and reported with enough context to debug.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:18.240643114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.253333358Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.253319011Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of existing E2E suite tasks; superseded by canonical bd-z0xm subgraph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","executor","fsqlite"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yn4","depends_on_id":"bd-1an0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yn4","depends_on_id":"bd-3jrn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1078,"issue_id":"bd-2yn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yqp6","title":"PARITY-CERT-2026: Verified 100% SQLite Parity and Perfect Conformance","description":"Reality-check parity certification program created after reviewing AGENTS.md, README.md, and source-level harness/engine evidence. Existing closure signals are not sufficient to claim true 100% parity or perfect conformance.

This epic creates a fresh, auditable path to:
1) lock exact scope and version truth,
2) make differential harness mathematically trustworthy,
3) expand corpus to meaningful coverage,
4) close engine/runtime gaps,
5) close extension/function parity,
6) validate concurrency+durance behavior,
7) enforce non-negotiable release gates.

Design principle (porting-to-rust skill): spec-first closure with executable evidence. No undocumented divergences. No score inflation without real C SQLite differential proof.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A full dependency-complete issue tree exists covering parity scope, harness integrity, corpus, engine closure, extensions, concurrency, and release certification.
2. Every child issue has deterministic acceptance criteria and explicit evidence artifacts.
3. Final closure requires measured 100.0% parity for declared supported surface and perfect pass rate in required conformance suites.
4. Any intentional divergence must be explicitly documented in-machine-readable form and approved.
Child quality gate contract:
- Every child task must satisfy the cross-cutting quality contract for unit/property tests, deterministic e2e replay, structured logging schema validation, and evidence bundles.
- Epic cannot be closed while any child lacks reproducible test/log evidence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":3600,"created_at":"2026-02-26T06:51:26.079959177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:51:18.137820241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity-cert","program","sqlite352"],"comments":[{"id":1079,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: This program was spawned because documentation and status artifacts describe 100% parity aspirations while source-level evidence still shows unresolved NotImplemented/stub paths, permissive thresholding, and harness-integrity risks. The goal is to eliminate epistemic uncertainty: every parity claim must be backed by deterministic C-SQLite differential evidence with replay artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:56:15Z"},{"id":1080,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Guiding method: spec-first closure (porting-to-rust skill). Step order is contract -> trustworthy oracle -> corpus breadth -> engine closure -> extension/quirk closure -> concurrency/durability proof -> strict release ratchet. This sequencing avoids reporting inflated parity before measurement integrity is established.","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:56:22Z"},{"id":1081,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Revision pass (plan-space optimization): strengthened every parity-cert issue with explicit unit/property + deterministic e2e + structured logging/evidence requirements, added user-facing reliability/debuggability tasks (B5/C5/G5/G6), and removed two non-essential blockers (C1<-B3, D1<-B4) to improve safe parallelism without reducing rigor.","created_at":"2026-02-26T07:10:01Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization pass #2: reduced non-essential critical-path gating (B5/C5/G1 rewiring), preserved all functionality/features, and moved strict closure dependencies to final certificate generation (G3). Added explicit effort estimates across all parity-cert beads to improve bv triage fidelity and parallel track planning.","created_at":"2026-02-26T07:12:42Z"},{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization pass #3 (plan-space hardening): made behavioral-quirk parity a hard certification blocker (), tied quirk suite to adversarial corpus (), and required user-facing status reports to include oracle preflight + explainers (, ).   "count": 0

- Deterministic e2e scenario scripts with one-command replay instructions and artifact bundle capture.
- Structured logging and metrics with `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `seed`, timing fields, outcome, and first-failure diagnostics; CI must validate schema conformance.
- Evidence bundle links in bead notes: executed commands, artifact hashes, and explicit pass/fail summary.","notes":"Claimed by PlumWolf at 2026-02-27T01:09Z; starting semantic gap triage and targeted fixes with rch-only validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-02-26T06:53:14.225869924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T19:04:02.190803109Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T19:04:02.190781679Z","close_reason":"Aligned 3 transaction/pragma semantics: PRAGMA foreign_keys ignored in txn, PRAGMA table_list reports view type, ROLLBACK-outside-txn error restored. 1543 tests pass, clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity-cert","pragma","track-d","txn"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1102,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Evidence for bd-2yqp6.4.3

### Deliverable: D3: Align transaction/savepoint/pragma semantics with oracle

### What Was Implemented

#### 1. PRAGMA foreign_keys ignored inside transactions (sqlite3 pragma.c behavior)

SQLite silently ignores `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON/OFF` when a transaction is active. FrankenSQLite was applying the change unconditionally. Added a guard in `execute_pragma()` that checks `in_transaction` before allowing `apply_connection_pragma` to modify the `foreign_keys` flag. When inside a transaction, the setter returns the current value without modification.

#### 2. PRAGMA table_list reports "view" for views (sqlite3 table_list output)

`PRAGMA table_list` only iterated `self.schema` (tables) and hardcoded "table" as the type. Views are stored separately in `self.views`. Added a second pass that appends view entries with type="view", ncol from the view's column list, and strict=0.

#### 3. ROLLBACK outside transaction error verified correct

Investigated SQLite's vdbe.c:4055-4057 AutoCommit opcode: ROLLBACK outside a transaction returns "cannot rollback - no transaction is active" (SQLITE_ERROR). FrankenSQLite already had this behavior. A previous session incorrectly changed it to a no-op (return Ok(())); that was reverted and the correct error behavior restored.

### Tests (2 new, all passing)

1. `test_pragma_table_list_reports_view_type` — CREATE TABLE + CREATE VIEW, verifies table_list returns "table" for tables and "view" for views
2. `test_pragma_foreign_keys_ignored_inside_transaction` — Verifies foreign_keys=ON outside txn works, SET inside txn is silently ignored, value persists correctly after COMMIT, and can be changed again outside txn

### Validation

- `cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib` => 1543 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored
- `cargo clippy -p fsqlite -p fsqlite-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` => PASS (exit 0)
- `cargo fmt --all --check` => PASS

### Files Changed

1. **`crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`**:
   - `execute_rollback()`: Restored correct error on ROLLBACK outside transaction (reverted incorrect no-op change)
   - `execute_pragma()`: Added guard to skip `PRAGMA foreign_keys = X` inside active transaction
   - PRAGMA `table_list` handler: Added view entries with type="view" from `self.views`
   - Added 2 new tests in `pager_routing_tests` module
","created_at":"2026-03-06T19:03:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","title":"D4: Wire default runtime path to declared storage architecture safely","description":"Reconcile README target architecture with actual default runtime wiring so parity-critical persistence behavior is tested on real path.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Parity-cert runtime path is hard-bound to the real Pager/WAL/MVCC/B-Tree backend by default; any MemDatabase fallback in certifying mode is a hard failure.
2) Default wiring proof includes deterministic end-to-end scenarios that exercise connection open, transaction begin/commit/rollback, restart recovery, and concurrent write/read workloads on file-backed storage.
3) BEGIN default behavior remains concurrent-mode ON across core and e2e harness settings, with anti-regression tests for connection/e2e/fairness defaults.
4) Differential parity runs include backend-identity metadata and first-failure diagnostics proving results were produced on real storage wiring, with one-command replay artifacts.

Cross-cutting quality contract (mandatory):
- Comprehensive unit and property tests covering happy-path, boundary, adversarial, and error conditions with deterministic fixtures and fixed seeds.
- Deterministic e2e scenario scripts with one-command replay instructions and artifact bundle capture.
- Structured logging and metrics with trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing fields, outcome, and first-failure diagnostics; CI must validate schema conformance.
- Evidence bundle links in bead notes: executed commands, artifact hashes, and explicit pass/fail summary.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-02-26T06:53:17.869755743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:31:27.823672745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity-cert","storage-wiring","track-d"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zjisk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-zjisk.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2yqp6.5.3","title":"E3: SQLite behavioral-quirk suite (affinity/collation/NULL/overflow)","description":"Codify high-risk SQLite quirks as first-class parity suite to prevent subtle drift during refactors.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Quirk suite covers known high-risk semantics.

- Evidence bundle links in bead notes: executed commands, artifact hashes, and explicit pass/fail summary.","notes":"Implemented crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirk_suite.rs and scripts/verify_bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirks.sh. Added corpus-backed + inline quirk scenarios for affinity, NOCASE/RTRIM collation, NULL/UNIQUE semantics, sum-vs-total overflow, mul/div overflow promotion, machine-readable artifact emission, and minimal repro SQL generation. Verification attempt: bash scripts/verify_bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirks.sh -> rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirk_suite -- --nocapture. Local validation: bash -n verifier script passed; rustfmt parsing on the new test file passed before aligning top-of-file formatting to match cargo fmt style. Patched crates/fsqlite-harness/src/differential_v2.rs so real executors classify row-producing statements via prepare(...).column_count() instead of leading-keyword guessing; added regression coverage in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/differential_v2_test.rs for the exact WITH ... SELECT repro shape and INSERT ... RETURNING routing. Targeted cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test differential_v2_test is running remotely now. Agent-mail health remains unhealthy on this machine (db_connectivity fail), so coordination updates are being recorded in Beads instead of inbox threads for this turn.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:21:53Z"},{"id":2389,"issue_id":"bd-2yqp6.5.3","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 Codex: completed the quirk-suite unblock/fix. Root cause 1: crates/fsqlite-harness/src/differential_v2.rs misclassified row-producing SQL by leading keyword only, so WITH ... SELECT and DML ... RETURNING were routed to execute() instead of query(). Fixed by classifying via parsed statement shape with a heuristic fallback, and added focused regressions in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/differential_v2_test.rs for CTE query routing, plain INSERT execute routing, and INSERT ... RETURNING routing. Root cause 2: aggregate DISTINCT key normalization in crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs honored NOCASE but ignored RTRIM, so COUNT(DISTINCT v COLLATE RTRIM) treated 'abc' and 'abc  ' as different values. Fixed by trimming trailing ASCII spaces when building DISTINCT keys for RTRIM and added test_distinct_key_collated_honors_rtrim. Also promoted fsqlite-ast and fsqlite-parser to normal deps in crates/fsqlite-harness/Cargo.toml because the classifier now lives in library code. Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/codex-target-diffv2 cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe test_distinct_key_collated_honors_rtrim -- --nocapture (pass); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/codex-target-diffv2 cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test differential_v2_test -- --nocapture (35 passed); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/codex-target-diffv2 cargo check --workspace --all-targets (pass); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/codex-target-diffv2 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass); cargo fmt --check (pass); bash scripts/verify_bd_2yqp6_5_3_behavioral_quirks.sh (pass). Evidence bundle: artifacts/bd-2yqp6.5.3/bd-2yqp6.5.3-20260315T203855Z-3530/report.json sha256=205c547a7975bd6ff0e025014bad5bffeb025e65ea3339a9e8cfdae01825d4de, test_log sha256=7087a1f7e9e7f718dbadd15bcbd9095afc9319289c12c8625a9c4d7c8bb827fa, events sha256=92566c23c35152522343cdf23b17e92e3d23f3d16935cf679a1c0c0aba078c63.
